dimensions.note: Makes no AWS calls and handles no secrets; it only consumes already-captured upstream audit data. The remediation-mapping change adds no credential path.
No logging calls changed; the source edits are a string returned by the report's remediationForKind helper and a model version/upgrade-chain bump.

remediationForKind keeps its exhaustive switch with a default arm; the new `network` case is added before default, so an unknown kind still falls through to the generic 'investigate' hint. The report's never-throw degraded-path behavior is unchanged.

Extended the existing unresolved-profile remediation test with a {kind:'network'} fixture asserting the new 'transient network failure — retry the scan' text renders, alongside the retained auth_expired/access_denied/other cases. The model-metadata test continues to assert model.version equals the manifest version and that the upgrade chain ends at model.version.
Pure rendering change; coalesce semantics and the model's write behavior are unchanged.
No direct external API client in this extension; it consumes swamp data/artifacts only.
No new files, handles, or clients; the changes are a one-line addition to a pure function plus a model version/upgrade-entry bump.
Manifest version (2026.06.26.1) matches the bumped model.version. No real identifiers introduced. The new test fixture uses placeholder profile labels (p-network) consistent with the existing ones.

MECHANICAL: no schema or writeResource payload changed. _lib/coverage.ts IamErrorSchema deliberately keeps `kind: z.string().default('other')` with .passthrough() because it coalesces multiple upstream lenses with differing kind vocabularies; it therefore already accepts the producer's new 'network' kind and ignores the extra `service` field. No new twin was added to this consumer (confirmed against TASK scope).
Resource lifetime and garbage-collection settings unchanged.
Read-only coalescer (single `coalesce` method) plus a workflow-scope report; no CRUD lifecycle surface is intended.
Non-mutating model; no pre-flight `check` labels apply, and none were changed.
MECHANICAL: no writeResource instance-name logic changed; coverage/summary naming is preserved.
Upstream data-read patterns are unchanged; the report still collects rows from the workflow's step executions and the model still reads via the data repository as before.

model.version bumped 2026.06.22.0 -> 2026.06.26.1 (required because the report source changed and the model-metadata test ties model.version to the manifest) with a matching no-op upgrades entry whose toVersion equals model.version; upgradeAttributes is the identity transform because no model schema or globalArguments changed.
